questionText: Você está numa reunião internacional e quer expressar que a sua empresa está comprometida com a redução das emissões de carbono a longo prazo. Qual frase transmite melhor esse comprometimento estratégico?
correctAnswer: We are fully committed to achieving net-zero carbon emissions by 2040 as a cornerstone of our long-term sustainability strategy.
options: We are fully committed to achieving net-zero carbon emissions by 2040 as a cornerstone of our long-term sustainability strategy.
We try to reduce carbon sometimes when it is convenient for our operations.
We have thought about lowering emissions and might do something about it soon.
Carbon reduction is one of many things we are considering for the future.

questionText: Em uma conferência ambiental, você quer elogiar os esforços de uma ONG que plantou um milhão de árvores. Qual expressão seria mais adequada e sofisticada?
correctAnswer: The reforestation initiative spearheaded by your organization is nothing short of remarkable and sets a compelling benchmark for others to follow.
options: The reforestation initiative spearheaded by your organization is nothing short of remarkable and sets a compelling benchmark for others to follow.
You planted a lot of trees and that is good.
Your tree planting was okay and we think it was a nice thing to do.
Congratulations for doing some environmental work recently.

questionText: Você precisa avisar colegas de trabalho para que apaguem as luzes ao sair das salas, como parte de uma campanha de sustentabilidade. Qual seria a forma mais natural e direta de fazer esse pedido em inglês?
correctAnswer: Please remember to switch off the lights when you leave a room — every small action counts toward our sustainability goals.
options: Please remember to switch off the lights when you leave a room — every small action counts toward our sustainability goals.
Lights must be switched off or else the planet will be destroyed immediately.
Do not forget that lights exist and they use energy sometimes.
It would be nice if some people maybe thought about the lights once in a while.

questionText: Durante uma palestra, o apresentador pede para a plateia definir o que é 'pegada de carbono'. Qual resposta seria a mais precisa e adequada ao nível C2?
correctAnswer: A carbon footprint refers to the total amount of greenhouse gases, particularly CO₂, emitted directly or indirectly by an individual, organization, or product.
options: A carbon footprint refers to the total amount of greenhouse gases, particularly CO₂, emitted directly or indirectly by an individual, organization, or product.
A carbon footprint is when you step on carbon and leave a mark on the ground.
Carbon footprint means the amount of coal a factory uses every year.
It is a term used to describe how much people like to recycle in their homes.

questionText: Você quer encorajar um amigo a começar a usar transporte público para ajudar o meio ambiente. Qual seria a abordagem mais persuasiva e natural em inglês?
correctAnswer: Switching to public transport is one of the most impactful individual choices you can make to reduce your personal carbon footprint.
options: Switching to public transport is one of the most impactful individual choices you can make to reduce your personal carbon footprint.
Buses exist and maybe you could take one if you want to.
Public transport is a thing and the environment might like it.
You should stop using your car because cars are bad and buses are good.

questionText: Em um e-mail corporativo, você precisa justificar o investimento em painéis solares na empresa. Qual argumento seria mais convincente e profissional?
correctAnswer: Installing solar panels will significantly reduce our operational energy costs while simultaneously demonstrating our commitment to renewable energy sources.
options: Installing solar panels will significantly reduce our operational energy costs while simultaneously demonstrating our commitment to renewable energy sources.
Solar panels are shiny and look good on the roof of the building.
We want solar panels because they are modern and people will see them.
Buying solar panels costs money but the sun is free so maybe it evens out.

questionText: Você precisa descrever o conceito de 'economia circular' para um público leigo em inglês. Qual descrição seria a mais clara e precisa?
correctAnswer: A circular economy is a regenerative system that aims to keep resources in use for as long as possible by reducing waste and continuously recycling materials.
options: A circular economy is a regenerative system that aims to keep resources in use for as long as possible by reducing waste and continuously recycling materials.
A circular economy is when money goes around in circles between companies.
Circular economy means that shops arrange their products in a circular pattern.
It is an economic theory about how circles are important in mathematics and business.

questionText: Você está apresentando um relatório de sustentabilidade e quer mencionar que a empresa reduziu o consumo de água em 30%. Qual frase seria mais adequada?
correctAnswer: Our operations achieved a 30% reduction in water consumption this fiscal year, reflecting our ongoing commitment to responsible resource management.
options: Our operations achieved a 30% reduction in water consumption this fiscal year, reflecting our ongoing commitment to responsible resource management.
We used less water this year by about 30% or so, which is pretty good we think.
Water was reduced somewhat and we are happy about the numbers we got.
30% of our water was stopped from being used and that happened during the year.

questionText: Em uma discussão sobre mudanças climáticas, você quer expressar urgência sobre a necessidade de ação imediata. Qual frase transmite melhor essa urgência?
correctAnswer: The window for meaningful climate action is rapidly closing, and further delay in implementing decisive policy measures will have irreversible consequences.
options: The window for meaningful climate action is rapidly closing, and further delay in implementing decisive policy measures will have irreversible consequences.
Climate change is a problem and people should do things about it at some point.
The weather has been changing and we probably need to think about it soon.
Climate issues are important and maybe governments will deal with them eventually.

questionText: Você quer recusar educadamente um produto com embalagem excessiva numa loja, explicando sua preocupação ambiental. Qual seria a resposta mais adequada?
correctAnswer: I'd prefer not to take the extra packaging, thank you — I try to minimize plastic waste whenever possible.
options: I'd prefer not to take the extra packaging, thank you — I try to minimize plastic waste whenever possible.
No packaging for me because plastic is evil and you should know better.
I refuse all packaging because I am an environmental activist and very serious.
Give me no plastic or I will complain about your store to everyone I know.

questionText: Em uma entrevista de emprego para uma empresa verde, você é perguntado sobre sua posição em relação à sustentabilidade. Qual resposta demonstra melhor alinhamento com valores ambientais?
correctAnswer: Sustainability isn't just a professional value for me — it's something I actively incorporate into my daily life, from composting to advocating for greener practices in my community.
options: Sustainability isn't just a professional value for me — it's something I actively incorporate into my daily life, from composting to advocating for greener practices in my community.
I like the environment and I think green things are nice to look at.
Sustainability is a word I have heard before and I think it sounds important.
I once recycled a bottle so I understand what your company is trying to do.

questionText: Você precisa propor uma moção formal em uma assembleia corporativa para adotar metas de emissões zero. Qual frase seria a mais adequada para abrir a proposta?
correctAnswer: I hereby propose that this organization formally commit to a science-based target of net-zero emissions by 2035, in alignment with the Paris Agreement.
options: I hereby propose that this organization formally commit to a science-based target of net-zero emissions by 2035, in alignment with the Paris Agreement.
I think we should try to not pollute so much and maybe write that down somewhere.
Someone should probably make a plan about emissions at some point this year.
Let us talk about emissions today and see if anyone has any interesting thoughts.

questionText: Você está escrevendo uma petição online sobre desmatamento na Amazônia. Qual chamada à ação seria mais eficaz e bem formulada?
correctAnswer: We urge policymakers to enact and enforce robust legislation to halt illegal deforestation and protect the Amazon's irreplaceable biodiversity.
options: We urge policymakers to enact and enforce robust legislation to halt illegal deforestation and protect the Amazon's irreplaceable biodiversity.
Please stop cutting trees in the Amazon because it is a very big forest and animals live there.
Trees are being cut and this is bad, so someone should do something to stop it.
The Amazon is important and we hope that governments will look into the tree situation.

questionText: Durante uma aula de inglês sobre meio ambiente, o professor pede para você explicar o que é 'greenwashing'. Qual resposta seria a mais precisa?
correctAnswer: Greenwashing refers to the practice of making misleading or unsubstantiated claims about the environmental benefits of a product, service, or company policy.
options: Greenwashing refers to the practice of making misleading or unsubstantiated claims about the environmental benefits of a product, service, or company policy.
Greenwashing is when you wash green vegetables before cooking them.
It is a special technique for cleaning buildings using environmentally safe soaps.
Greenwashing means painting your car green to show support for the environment.

questionText: Em um debate ambiental, você quer concordar com um colega que afirma que as energias renováveis são a solução mais viável. Qual resposta é mais sofisticada?
correctAnswer: I couldn't agree more. The scalability and declining cost of renewable technologies make them not only viable but arguably the most pragmatic pathway to a decarbonized economy.
options: I couldn't agree more. The scalability and declining cost of renewable technologies make them not only viable but arguably the most pragmatic pathway to a decarbonized economy.
Yes, I also think renewable energy is good and I agree with you completely.
Sure, green energy is nice and cheap and everyone should use it.
I agree. Renewable energy exists and it is better than not having any energy.

questionText: Você precisa recomendar práticas sustentáveis para uma escola primária. Qual sugestão seria mais clara e motivadora para crianças pequenas?
correctAnswer: Encourage students to bring reusable water bottles and lunchboxes to school — it's a fun and easy way to help keep our planet healthy.
options: Encourage students to bring reusable water bottles and lunchboxes to school — it's a fun and easy way to help keep our planet healthy.
Children should understand the full implications of single-use plastics on marine ecosystems and act accordingly.
Students must be trained in waste segregation protocols and lifecycle assessment methodologies.
Pupils are required to submit environmental impact reports on their daily consumption patterns.

questionText: Em uma reunião de condomínio, você quer sugerir a instalação de uma composteira coletiva. Qual argumento seria mais persuasivo para os moradores?
correctAnswer: A communal composting bin would divert organic waste from landfill, reduce our collective waste footprint, and even provide free fertilizer for our shared garden.
options: A communal composting bin would divert organic waste from landfill, reduce our collective waste footprint, and even provide free fertilizer for our shared garden.
Composting is fun and everyone should do it because dirt is important for flowers.
We could put food scraps somewhere and then use them for garden stuff maybe.
The compost thing would be good and I have seen it done on TV and it looked easy.

questionText: Você está escrevendo um artigo acadêmico e precisa formular uma hipótese sobre o impacto das políticas climáticas. Qual formulação é mais adequada ao registro acadêmico?
correctAnswer: This paper posits that carbon pricing mechanisms, when implemented at scale, exert a statistically significant downward pressure on industrial greenhouse gas emissions.
options: This paper posits that carbon pricing mechanisms, when implemented at scale, exert a statistically significant downward pressure on industrial greenhouse gas emissions.
We think that making companies pay for carbon will probably reduce pollution a bit.
Carbon taxes are good and this study will try to show that they work well.
Our research is about carbon prices and how they might affect emissions in some ways.

questionText: Num fórum internacional de política ambiental, você precisa rebater educadamente a posição de um delegado que minimiza os dados científicos sobre o aquecimento global. Qual resposta é mais eficaz e diplomática?
correctAnswer: With the utmost respect, the scientific consensus on anthropogenic climate change is unequivocal, and dismissing peer-reviewed evidence risks undermining the credibility of this forum's deliberations.
options: With the utmost respect, the scientific consensus on anthropogenic climate change is unequivocal, and dismissing peer-reviewed evidence risks undermining the credibility of this forum's deliberations.
You are wrong about climate change and scientists all agree that you are making a mistake.
I disagree with your point because the science is clear and everyone knows the planet is warming.
Climate science is settled and there is really no point in arguing about it anymore.

questionText: Em um relatório de auditoria ambiental, você precisa indicar que uma empresa violou os limites de emissões estabelecidos pela legislação. Qual formulação é mais precisa e formal?
correctAnswer: The audit findings indicate that the entity's reported emissions exceeded the regulatory thresholds stipulated under the Clean Air Framework by a margin of 18%, constituting a material non-compliance.
options: The audit findings indicate that the entity's reported emissions exceeded the regulatory thresholds stipulated under the Clean Air Framework by a margin of 18%, constituting a material non-compliance.
The company released too much pollution and broke the law by 18 percent.
Our audit found that the business was emitting more than it should and this is a problem.
The firm's emissions were higher than allowed and they need to fix this right away.

questionText: Você está mediando um conflito entre uma indústria e ativistas ambientais. Como você enquadraria o problema de forma neutra e construtiva para ambos os lados?
correctAnswer: Both parties share a legitimate stake in this outcome — industry requires regulatory certainty to plan investments, while environmental advocates rightly demand accountability for ecological impacts. Finding common ground here is not only possible but imperative.
options: Both parties share a legitimate stake in this outcome — industry requires regulatory certainty to plan investments, while environmental advocates rightly demand accountability for ecological impacts. Finding common ground here is not only possible but imperative.
The factory people want to keep working and the green people want to stop them, so they need to talk.
Industry and activists disagree and someone needs to tell them both to calm down and compromise.
This is a conflict between money and the environment and we need to pick the right side to support.

questionText: Em um painel de discussão, você precisa articular a diferença entre adaptação às mudanças climáticas e mitigação. Qual explicação é mais precisa e articulada?
correctAnswer: Mitigation refers to actions that reduce or prevent greenhouse gas emissions at source, whereas adaptation encompasses strategies that help societies and ecosystems cope with the impacts of climate change that are already locked in.
options: Mitigation refers to actions that reduce or prevent greenhouse gas emissions at source, whereas adaptation encompasses strategies that help societies and ecosystems cope with the impacts of climate change that are already locked in.
Mitigation is about stopping climate change and adaptation is about getting used to it.
Adapting means changing how we live and mitigating means trying to prevent problems from happening.
These are two words that mean similar things but one is more about the future and one is about now.

questionText: Você precisa redigir um parágrafo de conclusão para um relatório sobre biodiversidade, sintetizando os principais achados. Qual formulação é mais adequada?
correctAnswer: In summation, the evidence presented herein underscores the precipitous decline in global biodiversity, driven primarily by habitat destruction and climate disruption, and calls for an integrated, cross-sectoral response commensurate with the scale of the crisis.
options: In summation, the evidence presented herein underscores the precipitous decline in global biodiversity, driven primarily by habitat destruction and climate disruption, and calls for an integrated, cross-sectoral response commensurate with the scale of the crisis.
To conclude, biodiversity is declining fast because of human activities and we need to do something big.
In conclusion, the report shows that animals and plants are disappearing and this is a serious problem.
As a final point, the data in this report are about biodiversity loss and what might be done about it.

questionText: Em uma negociação de tratado ambiental, você precisa expressar uma concessão condicional — a seu país concordará com metas mais ambiciosas se os países desenvolvidos oferecerem suporte financeiro. Qual frase articula isso com mais precisão diplomática?
correctAnswer: Our delegation is prepared to endorse more ambitious emissions targets, contingent upon a binding commitment from developed nations to mobilize the climate finance pledged under the Paris Agreement.
options: Our delegation is prepared to endorse more ambitious emissions targets, contingent upon a binding commitment from developed nations to mobilize the climate finance pledged under the Paris Agreement.
We will accept higher targets if rich countries pay us the money they promised before.
Our country agrees to do more only if developed nations give us financial assistance first.
We might set higher goals, but we need developed countries to pay for it or we won't agree.

questionText: Você está apresentando um business case para uma diretoria sobre a transição para uma frota elétrica de veículos. Como você formularia a análise de custo-benefício de forma convincente?
correctAnswer: While the upfront capital expenditure for fleet electrification is considerable, the total cost of ownership over a ten-year horizon, factoring in reduced fuel and maintenance costs alongside available tax incentives, yields a compelling financial case.
options: While the upfront capital expenditure for fleet electrification is considerable, the total cost of ownership over a ten-year horizon, factoring in reduced fuel and maintenance costs alongside available tax incentives, yields a compelling financial case.
Electric cars cost more to buy but cost less to run, so over ten years we will save money.
The investment in electric vehicles is big but worth it because of fuel savings and tax breaks.
Buying electric cars is expensive at first but becomes cheaper later when you count all the costs.

questionText: Em um artigo de opinião sobre política climática, você quer criticar a lentidão dos governos sem soar alarmista. Qual abordagem retórica seria mais eficaz?
correctAnswer: One need not invoke apocalyptic scenarios to recognize that the measured, incremental pace of current climate policy falls woefully short of the systemic transformation that the science demands.
options: One need not invoke apocalyptic scenarios to recognize that the measured, incremental pace of current climate policy falls woefully short of the systemic transformation that the science demands.
Governments are too slow on climate change and will cause the end of the world if they don't hurry up.
Climate policy is bad and slow and governments are not doing enough, which is very concerning.
Governments should do more about climate change because scientists say it is an emergency.

questionText: Você precisa descrever o conceito de 'serviços ecossistêmicos' para um público de executivos de negócios. Qual definição seria mais estrategicamente enquadrada?
correctAnswer: Ecosystem services are the direct and indirect benefits that functioning natural systems provide to human welfare and economic activity — from water purification and pollination to carbon sequestration and climate regulation — many of which are currently unpriced by markets.
options: Ecosystem services are the direct and indirect benefits that functioning natural systems provide to human welfare and economic activity — from water purification and pollination to carbon sequestration and climate regulation — many of which are currently unpriced by markets.
Ecosystem services are when nature does useful things like cleaning water and helping bees pollinate flowers.
The ecosystem provides services that are like free benefits from nature that we often take for granted.
Natural systems do things that are helpful for people and business, and these are called ecosystem services.

questionText: Durante uma conferência de COP, você precisa pedir a palavra e fazer uma declaração formal em nome do seu país sobre metas de emissões. Como você abriria sua fala?
correctAnswer: Madam Chair, the delegation of Brazil wishes to place on record its strong support for the proposed enhanced nationally determined contributions framework and calls upon all parties to strengthen their commitments without further delay.
options: Madam Chair, the delegation of Brazil wishes to place on record its strong support for the proposed enhanced nationally determined contributions framework and calls upon all parties to strengthen their commitments without further delay.
Hello everyone, Brazil wants to say that we support the climate goals and think countries should do more.
I am from Brazil and I want to talk about the climate agreement and how we feel about the targets.
Brazil's position on this matter is positive and we think the framework is a good idea overall.

questionText: Você precisa explicar para um investidor estrangeiro os riscos financeiros associados às mudanças climáticas nos seus ativos. Qual abordagem é mais adequada ao contexto de finanças sustentáveis?
correctAnswer: Climate-related financial risks can be broadly categorized into transition risks — those arising from the shift to a low-carbon economy — and physical risks, which stem from the direct impacts of climate change on assets and operations.
options: Climate-related financial risks can be broadly categorized into transition risks — those arising from the shift to a low-carbon economy — and physical risks, which stem from the direct impacts of climate change on assets and operations.
Climate change can hurt your investments because of bad weather and because some industries will change.
There are financial risks from climate change that include floods and also changes in energy laws.
Your money could be affected by climate change in different ways that are related to pollution and weather.

questionText: Em um debate universitário, você precisa defender a posição de que o crescimento econômico e a sustentabilidade não são mutuamente exclusivos. Qual argumento seria mais robusto e sofisticado?
correctAnswer: The false dichotomy between economic growth and environmental sustainability ignores the mounting evidence that decoupling GDP growth from resource consumption is not only theoretically possible but increasingly empirically demonstrable in several advanced economies.
options: The false dichotomy between economic growth and environmental sustainability ignores the mounting evidence that decoupling GDP growth from resource consumption is not only theoretically possible but increasingly empirically demonstrable in several advanced economies.
Economy and environment can work together if we are smart about how we use resources.
Growing the economy and being sustainable are not opposites and we can have both if we try.
Some countries have shown that you can grow economically and still care about the environment.

questionText: Você está redigindo um brief de política pública sobre a introdução de um imposto sobre carbono. Qual frase introdutória é mais persuasiva e tecnicamente adequada?
correctAnswer: A well-designed carbon tax, calibrated to reflect the true social cost of carbon and revenue-recycled through dividend mechanisms, represents one of the most economically efficient tools available for driving large-scale emissions reductions.
options: A well-designed carbon tax, calibrated to reflect the true social cost of carbon and revenue-recycled through dividend mechanisms, represents one of the most economically efficient tools available for driving large-scale emissions reductions.
A carbon tax is a good idea because it makes companies pay for polluting, which encourages them to stop.
Taxing carbon is an efficient way to reduce emissions by putting a price on pollution.
Carbon taxes have been proven to work in some countries and could help reduce emissions significantly.

questionText: Numa conferência de alto nível, você precisa qualificar uma afirmação otimista sobre a transição energética global, reconhecendo os avanços mas apontando os obstáculos estruturais. Qual formulação é mais precisa e equilibrada?
correctAnswer: While the pace of renewable energy deployment is genuinely encouraging and deployment costs have fallen dramatically, the structural inertia embedded in fossil fuel-dependent infrastructure and political economies continues to impede the systemic transition that climate science demands.
options: While the pace of renewable energy deployment is genuinely encouraging and deployment costs have fallen dramatically, the structural inertia embedded in fossil fuel-dependent infrastructure and political economies continues to impede the systemic transition that climate science demands.
Renewable energy is growing fast and getting cheaper, but fossil fuels are still a big problem for the transition.
Things are getting better with clean energy, but we still have many challenges ahead before we can fully transition.
The energy transition is going well in some ways, but there are still issues with oil and gas that need to be fixed.

questionText: Em um paper acadêmico sobre justiça climática, você precisa articular o princípio de 'responsabilidades comuns porém diferenciadas' no contexto das negociações da UNFCCC. Qual formulação é epistemicamente mais rigorosa?
correctAnswer: The CBDR principle acknowledges the asymmetric historical contribution of industrialized nations to atmospheric greenhouse gas concentrations while recognizing that all states bear a duty to act, albeit in proportion to their capacities and developmental imperatives.
options: The CBDR principle acknowledges the asymmetric historical contribution of industrialized nations to atmospheric greenhouse gas concentrations while recognizing that all states bear a duty to act, albeit in proportion to their capacities and developmental imperatives.
CBDR means that rich countries should do more because they caused more pollution historically.
The principle says developed countries have more responsibility because they industrialized first and emitted more.
Common but differentiated responsibilities means that all countries must act on climate but wealthier ones must do more.

questionText: Você está redigindo o sumário executivo de um relatório do IPCC e precisa comunicar os limites de confiança de projeções climáticas sem comprometer a clareza para tomadores de decisão. Qual frase é mais exemplar?
correctAnswer: Projections indicate with high confidence that global mean surface temperatures will exceed 1.5°C above pre-industrial levels before mid-century under all assessed emissions scenarios, though the timing and regional distribution of impacts remain subject to substantial uncertainty.
options: Projections indicate with high confidence that global mean surface temperatures will exceed 1.5°C above pre-industrial levels before mid-century under all assessed emissions scenarios, though the timing and regional distribution of impacts remain subject to substantial uncertainty.
Scientists are very sure that temperatures will rise more than 1.5°C soon, but they are not sure exactly when or where.
It is highly likely that the world will warm by 1.5°C or more in the near future, depending on emissions.
Climate models predict warming above 1.5°C with great confidence, but regional details are still uncertain.

questionText: Em uma negociação de alto risco sobre um mecanismo de precificação de carbono transfronteiriço, você precisa rejeitar uma proposta sem fechar a porta para futuras negociações. Qual resposta demonstra mais habilidade diplomática e nuance linguística?
correctAnswer: While our delegation finds merit in the underlying intent of the proposed mechanism, we are not in a position to endorse its current formulation, and would respectfully suggest that a joint technical working group be convened to explore modifications that could garner broader consensus.
options: While our delegation finds merit in the underlying intent of the proposed mechanism, we are not in a position to endorse its current formulation, and would respectfully suggest that a joint technical working group be convened to explore modifications that could garner broader consensus.
We cannot accept this proposal as it is, but we are willing to keep talking about it to find a solution.
The idea has some good points but we disagree with it and think it needs to be changed before we can agree.
Our country does not support this mechanism right now, but we remain open to discussion about alternatives.

questionText: Você precisa desafiar a premissa implícita de um colega que argumenta que as soluções tecnológicas por si só resolverão a crise climática, sem abordar padrões de consumo. Qual intervenção é mais intelectualmente rigorosa?
correctAnswer: The techno-optimist framing, while intellectually appealing, elides a fundamental tension: technological efficiency gains have historically been offset by rebound effects, suggesting that decarbonization requires not merely cleaner production but a structural rethinking of consumption norms.
options: The techno-optimist framing, while intellectually appealing, elides a fundamental tension: technological efficiency gains have historically been offset by rebound effects, suggesting that decarbonization requires not merely cleaner production but a structural rethinking of consumption norms.
Technology is helpful but it alone won't solve climate change because people also need to consume less.
Relying only on technology is not enough because when things become more efficient, people tend to use even more.
Technology can improve efficiency but history shows that people usually end up consuming more, so behavior must change too.

questionText: Em um seminário sobre finanças sustentáveis, você precisa diferenciar as taxonomias de risco climático físico entre riscos agudos e crônicos para um público de analistas financeiros sêniores. Qual distinção é mais tecnicamente precisa?
correctAnswer: Acute physical risks denote discrete, event-driven climate hazards such as extreme weather events with well-characterized loss distributions, whereas chronic physical risks encompass gradual, long-term shifts in climate variables — rising sea levels, shifting precipitation patterns — that incrementally erode asset values and productive capacity.
options: Acute physical risks denote discrete, event-driven climate hazards such as extreme weather events with well-characterized loss distributions, whereas chronic physical risks encompass gradual, long-term shifts in climate variables — rising sea levels, shifting precipitation patterns — that incrementally erode asset values and productive capacity.
Acute risks are sudden events like floods while chronic risks are slower changes like rising sea levels that happen over time.
Short-term physical risks include storms and floods, while long-term risks include gradual changes in climate that affect assets.
Acute climate risks are immediate disasters and chronic risks are ongoing environmental changes that slowly damage investments.

questionText: Você está elaborando uma análise crítica de um estudo que defende o geoengenhamento solar como solução para as mudanças climáticas. Qual objeção é mais epistemicamente fundamentada?
correctAnswer: The governance lacunae surrounding solar radiation management are particularly troubling: unilateral deployment by any single actor could impose non-consensual climatic consequences on sovereign states, raising profound questions of international law and distributive justice that the study conspicuously fails to address.
options: The governance lacunae surrounding solar radiation management are particularly troubling: unilateral deployment by any single actor could impose non-consensual climatic consequences on sovereign states, raising profound questions of international law and distributive justice that the study conspicuously fails to address.
Solar geoengineering is dangerous because one country could use it without asking others and change the climate for everyone.
The study ignores the political problems with solar geoengineering, like who decides when and how to use it.
Geoengineering could be used by powerful countries without consent of others, which raises serious legal and ethical issues.

questionText: Num artigo de opinião, você precisa desconstruir o argumento de que a proteção ambiental necessariamente sacrifica empregos industriais, utilizando evidências empíricas. Qual refutação é mais convincente e sofisticada?
correctAnswer: The jobs-versus-environment binary, long deployed as a rhetorical bulwark against regulation, is increasingly belied by empirical evidence: the renewable energy sector now employs more workers globally than fossil fuel industries, and green infrastructure investment consistently generates positive employment multipliers.
options: The jobs-versus-environment binary, long deployed as a rhetorical bulwark against regulation, is increasingly belied by empirical evidence: the renewable energy sector now employs more workers globally than fossil fuel industries, and green infrastructure investment consistently generates positive employment multipliers.
The argument that green policies kill jobs is wrong because renewable energy creates many jobs too.
Evidence shows that environmental protection does not destroy jobs; in fact, clean energy employs more people than fossil fuels now.
Green jobs exist and the renewable sector is growing, so protecting the environment does not have to cost jobs.

questionText: Em um depoimento perante uma comissão parlamentar sobre política energética, você precisa argumentar contra subsídios a combustíveis fósseis com base em externalidades não precificadas. Qual argumento é mais tecnicamente robusto?
correctAnswer: The continued provision of explicit and implicit subsidies to fossil fuel industries represents a profound market distortion: it systematically underprices carbon-intensive energy by externalizing the social costs of emissions — estimated by the IMF at upwards of $5 trillion annually — onto present and future generations.
options: The continued provision of explicit and implicit subsidies to fossil fuel industries represents a profound market distortion: it systematically underprices carbon-intensive energy by externalizing the social costs of emissions — estimated by the IMF at upwards of $5 trillion annually — onto present and future generations.
Fossil fuel subsidies are unfair because they make dirty energy cheaper and don't include the environmental damage in the price.
Subsidizing oil and gas distorts the market because the real costs of pollution are not included in the price consumers pay.
Governments should stop subsidizing fossil fuels because this makes renewable energy less competitive and ignores the cost of pollution.

questionText: Você precisa articular, em um contexto de litigância climática, o nexo causal entre as emissões históricas de uma empresa específica e os danos sofridos por comunidades costeiras vulneráveis. Qual formulação é mais juridicamente precisa?
correctAnswer: Attribution science has advanced sufficiently to establish, on the balance of probabilities, that the defendant's cumulative greenhouse gas emissions materially contributed to the observed increase in sea level that caused the quantifiable harm suffered by the plaintiff communities.
options: Attribution science has advanced sufficiently to establish, on the balance of probabilities, that the defendant's cumulative greenhouse gas emissions materially contributed to the observed increase in sea level that caused the quantifiable harm suffered by the plaintiff communities.
Science can now prove that the company's pollution caused sea levels to rise and damaged coastal communities.
We can show that the company's emissions contributed to the sea level rise that harmed the communities suing them.
Attribution research links the company's greenhouse gases to rising seas and the damages that coastal residents experienced.

questionText: Em um artigo de revisão sobre os limites planetários, você precisa explicar por que a ultrapassagem simultânea de múltiplos limites pode gerar efeitos não lineares e cascatas de risco. Qual explicação é mais científica e rigorosa?
correctAnswer: When multiple planetary boundaries are transgressed concurrently, the resultant interactions between subsystems — such as permafrost thaw amplifying atmospheric methane concentrations, which in turn accelerates ice sheet destabilization — can trigger non-linear feedbacks that rapidly propel Earth systems toward tipping points from which recovery is not guaranteed.
options: When multiple planetary boundaries are transgressed concurrently, the resultant interactions between subsystems — such as permafrost thaw amplifying atmospheric methane concentrations, which in turn accelerates ice sheet destabilization — can trigger non-linear feedbacks that rapidly propel Earth systems toward tipping points from which recovery is not guaranteed.
Crossing several planetary limits at once is very dangerous because different environmental problems can make each other worse.
When we break multiple planetary boundaries simultaneously, the different systems interact and can cause runaway effects that are hard to stop.
Breaking too many planetary boundaries at the same time creates feedback loops that can push Earth's systems past points of no return.

questionText: Você precisa responder a um cético que argumenta que investir em adaptação climática em países em desenvolvimento é economicamente inviável. Qual contra-argumento é mais persuasivo e empiricamente fundamentado?
correctAnswer: The framing of adaptation investment as a fiscal burden ignores its function as a risk management instrument: the World Bank's estimates suggest that every dollar invested in climate-resilient infrastructure in developing economies yields between $2 and $10 in avoided losses from climate-related disasters.
options: The framing of adaptation investment as a fiscal burden ignores its function as a risk management instrument: the World Bank's estimates suggest that every dollar invested in climate-resilient infrastructure in developing economies yields between $2 and $10 in avoided losses from climate-related disasters.
Investing in climate adaptation in developing countries is actually worth it because it saves more money in disaster costs.
The World Bank says that adaptation investment in poor countries pays off because it prevents expensive climate disasters.
Climate adaptation spending in developing nations is cost-effective because it reduces future losses from extreme weather events.

questionText: Em uma audiência de alto nível sobre biodiversidade marinha, você precisa descrever as consequências da acidificação oceânica sobre os ecossistemas de recifes de coral com precisão científica. Qual explicação é mais acurada?
correctAnswer: Ocean acidification, driven by the absorption of anthropogenic CO₂ emissions, reduces seawater pH and diminishes the saturation state of aragonite, progressively impairing the calcification processes upon which reef-building corals and other calcifying organisms depend for skeletal formation.
options: Ocean acidification, driven by the absorption of anthropogenic CO₂ emissions, reduces seawater pH and diminishes the saturation state of aragonite, progressively impairing the calcification processes upon which reef-building corals and other calcifying organisms depend for skeletal formation.
When oceans absorb CO₂, they become more acidic, which makes it harder for corals to build their skeletons and can kill reefs.
CO₂ dissolves in seawater to create acid, which damages coral reefs by preventing them from forming properly.
Ocean acidification from CO₂ emissions makes the water more acidic, which weakens and destroys coral reef structures over time.

questionText: Você está participando de uma sessão de negociação sobre mecanismos de transferência de tecnologia para países em desenvolvimento no contexto climático. Você precisa rebater o argumento de que a proteção de patentes impede essa transferência. Qual posição é mais juridicamente e politicamente fundamentada?
correctAnswer: While intellectual property protections do create market barriers to technology diffusion, the empirical evidence on their net effect on clean technology transfer is equivocal; voluntary licensing frameworks, publicly-funded R&D, and concessional finance mechanisms may offer more tractable pathways than compulsory licensing, which risks deterring future innovation investment.
options: While intellectual property protections do create market barriers to technology diffusion, the empirical evidence on their net effect on clean technology transfer is equivocal; voluntary licensing frameworks, publicly-funded R&D, and concessional finance mechanisms may offer more tractable pathways than compulsory licensing, which risks deterring future innovation investment.
Patents do block technology transfer but there are other ways to share clean technology that would work better.
IP protection is a barrier but forcing companies to share their technology could reduce future investment in clean innovation.
The evidence on patents and technology transfer is mixed, and compulsory licensing may do more harm than good to innovation.

questionText: Em um programa de rádio sobre meio ambiente, o apresentador pergunta por que muitos países em desenvolvimento resistem a metas climáticas mais rígidas. Como você explicaria essa tensão de forma justa e equilibrada?
correctAnswer: Many developing nations argue, with considerable justification, that they bear a disproportionate burden of climate impacts while having contributed minimally to cumulative emissions, and that stringent mitigation targets without adequate financial and technological support would effectively constrain their development trajectories.
options: Many developing nations argue, with considerable justification, that they bear a disproportionate burden of climate impacts while having contributed minimally to cumulative emissions, and that stringent mitigation targets without adequate financial and technological support would effectively constrain their development trajectories.
Developing countries resist climate targets because they want to keep growing economically and don't think it's fair.
Poor countries don't want strict climate rules because they feel rich countries caused the problem and should fix it first.
Developing nations oppose tough climate targets because they say it's unfair to limit their growth when they didn't cause the problem.

questionText: Você precisa recomendar, em uma carta formal, que uma câmara municipal adote uma política de compras verdes. Qual argumentação é mais adequada ao registro formal?
correctAnswer: We respectfully submit that the adoption of a comprehensive green procurement policy would not only align the municipality's purchasing practices with its stated environmental commitments but would also stimulate local demand for sustainable goods and services, generating co-benefits for the regional economy.
options: We respectfully submit that the adoption of a comprehensive green procurement policy would not only align the municipality's purchasing practices with its stated environmental commitments but would also stimulate local demand for sustainable goods and services, generating co-benefits for the regional economy.
We think the city should buy green products because it would help the environment and the local economy.
A green procurement policy would be good for the municipality and for the environment, so we recommend it.
The city council should consider buying sustainable products to match its environmental goals and support local businesses.

questionText: Em uma entrevista para um podcast ambiental, você quer expressar ceticismo construtivo sobre as promessas de carbono zero feitas pelas grandes corporações. Qual formulação equilibra a crítica com o rigor analítico?
correctAnswer: Corporate net-zero pledges are not without merit, but their credibility is heavily contingent on the robustness of the underlying decarbonization pathways — many current commitments rely excessively on offset mechanisms rather than genuine emissions reductions at source.
options: Corporate net-zero pledges are not without merit, but their credibility is heavily contingent on the robustness of the underlying decarbonization pathways — many current commitments rely excessively on offset mechanisms rather than genuine emissions reductions at source.
Companies say they will reach net zero but often use carbon offsets instead of actually reducing their emissions.
Corporate climate promises sound good but many rely on buying carbon credits rather than real emission cuts.
Net-zero commitments from big companies are often not genuine because they use offsets to avoid cutting real emissions.

questionText: Você está escrevendo a introdução de um manifesto ambiental e quer captar a urgência existencial da crise climática com profundidade retórica. Qual abertura é mais evocativa e impactante?
correctAnswer: We stand at an inflection point in Earth's history — one of our own making — where the choices inscribed in policy, technology, and conscience over the next decade will determine the habitability of this planet for all generations that follow.
options: We stand at an inflection point in Earth's history — one of our own making — where the choices inscribed in policy, technology, and conscience over the next decade will determine the habitability of this planet for all generations that follow.
Climate change is very serious and we need to act now before it is too late for everyone on Earth.
The environmental crisis is real and urgent, and the decisions we make today will affect future generations.
We are living through an important moment in history for the environment and we must make good choices.

questionText: Em uma sessão de treino para jovens diplomatas, você precisa demonstrar como expressar forte discordância com a posição de um país sem violar os protocolos diplomáticos. Qual formulação é mais exemplar?
correctAnswer: Our delegation notes, with profound concern, that the position articulated by the distinguished representative appears to be at variance with the body of scientific evidence and the principles enshrined in the Framework Convention, and we respectfully urge a reconsideration.
options: Our delegation notes, with profound concern, that the position articulated by the distinguished representative appears to be at variance with the body of scientific evidence and the principles enshrined in the Framework Convention, and we respectfully urge a reconsideration.
We strongly disagree with what that country just said and think they are ignoring the science.
The other delegation is wrong about this and they should read the scientific evidence more carefully.
We are very concerned about the position taken by the other country and hope they will change their mind.

questionText: Você precisa explicar, para uma audiência de economistas, por que o mercado não resolve sozinho o problema das externalidades ambientais negativas. Qual explicação é mais tecnicamente correta?
correctAnswer: Environmental externalities represent a canonical market failure: when the social costs of pollution are not internalized by producers, market prices systematically understate the true cost of production, leading to allocative inefficiency and overconsumption of pollution-intensive goods relative to the social optimum.
options: Environmental externalities represent a canonical market failure: when the social costs of pollution are not internalized by producers, market prices systematically understate the true cost of production, leading to allocative inefficiency and overconsumption of pollution-intensive goods relative to the social optimum.
Markets fail to address pollution because companies don't pay for the environmental damage they cause, so products are priced too cheaply.
The market doesn't solve environmental problems because polluters don't pay the full cost of their emissions, leading to too much pollution.
Environmental damage is a market failure because the costs of pollution are not included in prices, so polluting goods are overproduced.

questionText: Em uma reunião de cúpula sobre florestas tropicais, você precisa propor um mecanismo de compensação para países que preservam florestas em vez de desmatá-las. Qual proposta é mais técnica e persuasiva?
correctAnswer: We propose the establishment of a results-based payment mechanism under which host countries receive internationally verified compensation calibrated to measured, reported, and verified reductions in deforestation and forest degradation, providing a compelling alternative income stream to land conversion.
options: We propose the establishment of a results-based payment mechanism under which host countries receive internationally verified compensation calibrated to measured, reported, and verified reductions in deforestation and forest degradation, providing a compelling alternative income stream to land conversion.
Countries that keep their forests should be paid for not cutting them down so they have a financial reason to protect them.
We should create a payment system where countries receive money for reducing deforestation, which is verified internationally.
A fund that pays countries to preserve forests instead of clearing them would give governments an economic incentive to protect biodiversity.

questionText: Você precisa comunicar, numa nota técnica, que o modelo climático atual não captura adequadamente os feedbacks de vapor d'água em altas latitudes. Qual formulação é mais apropriada?
correctAnswer: The current model generation exhibits systematic biases in its representation of water vapour feedbacks at high latitudes, potentially underestimating regional amplification of warming and introducing non-trivial uncertainties into projected precipitation changes in Arctic and sub-Arctic regions.
options: The current model generation exhibits systematic biases in its representation of water vapour feedbacks at high latitudes, potentially underestimating regional amplification of warming and introducing non-trivial uncertainties into projected precipitation changes in Arctic and sub-Arctic regions.
The climate model has problems with water vapour in polar regions, which means it may underestimate warming there.
Current models don't accurately represent water vapour feedbacks at high latitudes, leading to uncertainty in Arctic warming projections.
There are known errors in how models simulate water vapour near the poles, which affects how accurately they predict warming and rainfall.

questionText: Em um contexto de advocacia ambiental corporativa, você precisa convencer um CEO cético de que o relatório de sustentabilidade integrado com o relatório financeiro não é apenas uma obrigação regulatória, mas uma vantagem competitiva. Qual argumento é mais estrategicamente eficaz?
correctAnswer: Integrated reporting is increasingly influencing capital allocation decisions among institutional investors: companies that demonstrate rigorous, forward-looking ESG disclosure tend to command lower cost of capital, attract longer-term investors, and exhibit greater resilience to regulatory and reputational shocks.
options: Integrated reporting is increasingly influencing capital allocation decisions among institutional investors: companies that demonstrate rigorous, forward-looking ESG disclosure tend to command lower cost of capital, attract longer-term investors, and exhibit greater resilience to regulatory and reputational shocks.
Publishing a sustainability report is good for your image and can help attract investors who care about ESG.
Integrated sustainability reporting helps companies look better to investors and can reduce their cost of borrowing.
ESG reporting is becoming important for investors and companies that do it well tend to perform better financially.

questionText: Você está moderando um painel sobre a ética da geoengenharia e precisa formular uma pergunta que provoque reflexão profunda sobre a agência humana e os limites da intervenção tecnológica na natureza. Qual pergunta é mais filosoficamente provocativa?
correctAnswer: Does humanity's demonstrated capacity to alter planetary systems confer upon us a corresponding moral obligation to deploy that capacity in service of stabilization, or does the precautionary principle demand that we exercise restraint precisely because our ability to model the consequences of intervention remains profoundly limited?
options: Does humanity's demonstrated capacity to alter planetary systems confer upon us a corresponding moral obligation to deploy that capacity in service of stabilization, or does the precautionary principle demand that we exercise restraint precisely because our ability to model the consequences of intervention remains profoundly limited?
Should we use geoengineering to fix climate change, or is it too risky because we don't fully understand what would happen?
Is it ethical to use technology to engineer the climate when we cannot predict all the consequences of doing so?
Do humans have the right to intentionally change the climate system, and what moral responsibilities come with that power?
